15th Annual Charity Ride · Eganville, Ontario

Tour De Bonnechere

📅  Sunday, August 30th · Eganville, Ontario

Cycle the stunning Ottawa Valley and help raise funds for the Eganville Rotary Mental Health Project and the Eganville Foodbank.

The Routes

Choose Your Challenge

Five routes through the beautiful Ottawa Valley. River views, heritage farms and the infamous Cormac Hill if you dare — something for everyone. Please note the routes linked below are from 2025 and may change slightly. Any changes will be shared with riders by email and reflected on event day in both our RideWithGPS library and our printed event day maps.

🗺️ Interactive Route Maps
Family

Family Cave Coaster

17.5 km

A flat out-and-back on quiet rural roads to the Bonnechere Caves, following alongside the beautiful Bonnechere River. Perfect for families and first-timers.

View Route ↗
Gravel

Gravel Grinder

50 km

A favourite among local gravel riders. Turns, curves and swerves with a few ups and downs. Pavement at the beginning and end — but like an Oreo, the goodness is in the middle. Pass by marshlands, old farms and bush.

View Route ↗
Challenge

Bonnechere Beauty

67 km

A paved ride through scenic countryside with numerous long, straight and flat stretches — with a few easy hills to keep the legs pumping.

View Route ↗
Epic

Bonnechere Beauty EZ

100 km

A lovely, flatish century ride that travels many of the same roads as the 67km Bonnechere Beauty. Few vehicles, rich agricultural land, weathered barns and the Bonnechere River.

View Route ↗
Beast Mode

Monarch of the Mountains

100 km

A challenging 100km ride. Use the first 20k to warm up before ascending the infamous Cormac Hill to Foymount — the highest-populated spot in Ontario. Tackle the locals' notorious "quad buster" through Quadeville and the hilly Letterkenny road, then enjoy a glorious 30k roll down Opeongo Line back into Eganville.

View Route ↗
Volunteer

Be Part Of The Crew

No ride happens without amazing volunteers. Join the team behind the scenes and help make this event unforgettable.

🚦
Route Marshall
Keep riders on track and safe at key intersections and turn points along all routes.
🍌
Rest Stop Crew
Staff water and nutrition stations, cheering riders on and keeping energy high.
🔧
Bike Mechanic
On-call mechanical support for riders who need a quick fix before or during the ride.
📸
Photography
Capture the spirit of the ride — on course and at the start/finish area.
🎪
Event Setup & Finish
Help set up the festival area, direct traffic, and assist at the finish line celebration.

Sign Up To Volunteer

Fill out the form and we'll be in touch with shift details closer to event day.
